purpose: Test whether smaller mHC token chunks solve the MBS8 corrected-mHC OOR without changing the model or sparse/MoE settings.

post_failure_node_state.training_processes: none found by pgrep torchrun|torchtitan/train.py after failure
post_failure_node_state.read: ROCm SMI still reported high VRAM on several GPUs with no TorchTitan KFD process after the abort, so do not launch another memory-fit gate until the node is cleaned or accounting settles.

source_read.amd_current_path: PyTorch mHC helpers in torchtitan/models/deepseek_v4/model.py with optional torch checkpoint and token chunking.
source_read.lesson: Ascend does mHC as custom autograd kernels for pre, post, head, and Sinkhorn backward; AMD currently only reduced one forward materialization and still pays PyTorch autograd/lifetime costs.

read: Chunk512 does not solve MBS8 corrected-mHC fit. It fails after one completed metric step with HSA out-of-resources and 0 MB free.
Chunk512 step-1 throughput is lower than chunk1024 step-1 and it does not reach the chunk1024 three-step fit smoke.

This rejects further chunk-size-only probing as the main mHC path. The next useful implementation target is an AMD mHC custom-autograd/kernel backend modelled on Ascend MHCPreTriton/MHCPostTriton, or another source-level reduction of saved mHC lifetime.
decision: Do not promote chunk512 and do not continue blind chunk-size sweeps. Keep MBS4 attention_and_hyper_connections as corrected mHC baseline while porting/fusing the mHC pre/post/head autograd boundary.
